title: atalantia missionis
intro: Atalantia missionis, also known as the Calamondin or Missionary Plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of vitamin C, flavonoids, and limonoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Atalantia missionis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used to make jams, preserves, and other food products.
